context: Go 1.24 CLI/TUI code lives under internal/, with shared PRD state and validation in internal/shared/prd. The web UI is a Vite + React + TypeScript app in web/src, the run detail page loads PRDs from GET /api/runs/{id}/prd through web/src/hooks/usePRDLoader.ts, and the implementation story list is rendered by web/src/components/StoryProgressPanel.tsx inside web/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx. The TUI implementation view lives in internal/tui/view.go and already uses getStatusIcon/getStatusText in internal/tui/styles.go. The backend run response already exposes story_progress with per-story completed_slices, total_slices, and per-slice passes flags in internal/web/handlers/runs.go. Run the focused tests with go test ./internal/shared/prd ./internal/tui ./internal/web/handlers && cd web && npm test.
test_spec: Verify that Story.NextPendingSlice, Story.CompletedSliceCount, and Story.AllSlicesPassed agree for mixed, empty, and fully passed stories in internal/shared/prd/slice_test.go. Verify that web/src/components/StoryProgressPanel.tsx renders completed, in progress, and pending labels for slices in the current story and that web/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx still keeps the current story expanded and the toolbar counts unchanged. Verify that internal/tui/view.go renders each slice under the active story with completed, in progress, and pending status text and icons while keeping the active story highlight behavior unchanged.

stories.title: Deriving slice states
stories.description: Keep slice progress derivation in internal/shared/prd/types.go and prove it with internal/shared/prd/slice_test.go. A story's first unfinished slice is the in-progress slice, earlier passed slices are completed, and later unfinished slices are pending. Story.NextPendingSlice, Story.CompletedSliceCount, and Story.AllSlicesPassed must agree for mixed, empty, and fully passed stories.

slices.behavior: A mixed story exposes exactly one in-progress slice, with earlier slices completed and later slices pending.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing test in internal/shared/prd/slice_test.go that builds a story with one passed slice, one unfinished slice, and one untouched slice and asserts NextPendingSlice returns the first unfinished slice and CompletedSliceCount returns 1.
slices.refactor_hint: Keep the state derivation in one place only if the tests show duplicated logic.

slices.behavior: An empty story reports no in-progress slice and zero completed slices.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing test in internal/shared/prd/slice_test.go for a story with zero slices and assert NextPendingSlice returns nil and CompletedSliceCount returns 0.
slices.refactor_hint: Reuse the same empty-story assertions anywhere else the code needs them.

slices.behavior: A fully passed story reports no in-progress slice and AllSlicesPassed returns true.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing test in internal/shared/prd/slice_test.go for a story whose slices all pass and assert NextPendingSlice returns nil, CompletedSliceCount equals len(Slices), and AllSlicesPassed returns true.
slices.refactor_hint: Reuse the same completed-story assertions anywhere else the code needs them.

stories.title: Rendering slice progress in web
stories.description: Update web/src/components/StoryProgressPanel.tsx so each slice row renders a visible completed, in progress, or pending label alongside behavior, red hint, and optional refactor hint. Keep web/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx showing the same toolbar counts and current-story expansion while it passes the PRD document from web/src/hooks/usePRDLoader.ts into the panel.

slices.behavior: The web story progress panel shows completed, in-progress, and pending labels for each slice in the current story.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing Vitest case in web/src/components/StoryProgressPanel.test.tsx that renders a PRD with passed, current, and untouched slices and expects the three labels plus the existing behavior and red-hint text.
slices.refactor_hint: Factor any label formatting into a local helper if it keeps the list markup simple.

slices.behavior: RunDetail keeps the story summary counts unchanged while the per-slice states are visible.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing test in web/src/pages/RunDetail.test.tsx that loads a PRD with mixed slice states and asserts the toolbar count stays at completed/total while the panel renders the slice labels.
slices.refactor_hint: Let StoryProgressPanel derive its own labels from the PRD data instead of duplicating slice state in RunDetail.

slices.behavior: RunDetail keeps the current story expanded while the per-slice states are visible.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing test in web/src/pages/RunDetail.test.tsx that loads a PRD with mixed slice states and asserts the current story details element remains open.
slices.refactor_hint: Keep the current-story expansion logic inside StoryProgressPanel rather than copying it into RunDetail.

stories.title: Rendering slice progress in TUI
stories.description: Update internal/tui/view.go so renderImplementationStory shows each slice underneath the active story with completed, in progress, and pending status using the existing getStatusIcon and getStatusText helpers in internal/tui/styles.go. Keep the story-row highlight logic unchanged and cover the active story plus slice rows in internal/tui/view_test.go.

slices.behavior: The TUI implementation view prints each slice in the current story with a visible completed, in-progress, or pending status.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing internal/tui/view_test.go case that renders PhaseImplementation with mixed slice progress and expects the slice status text and icons under the active story.
slices.refactor_hint: Reuse the existing status icon and text helpers instead of inventing a second styling path.

slices.behavior: The current story row stays highlighted while its slice list renders.
slices.red_hint: Add a failing internal/tui/view_test.go case that renders a current story and a non-current story and asserts the selected styling stays on the current story row after slice rows are added.
slices.refactor_hint: Keep the story highlight logic separate from the slice rows so the two concerns do not drift.
